rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINDA YEE LIN MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/14/2022. Continued again same issues. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please prove the estate is entirely community property of Decedent. 2) Decedent died 08/29/2016. Unclear when account begins as page 4 line 2-3 alleges the account begins 08/28/2016 the summary of account alleges the beginning assets on hand on 08/28/2016 and Schedule A alleges beginning assets on hand 08/29/2016. A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. (See LRSF 14.34). 3) Petition must be signed and verified by Petitioner. All pleadings filed in proceedings under the Probate Code must be verified. If two or more persons join in a pleading, it may be verified by any of them. (See CRC 7.103). 4) The petition alleges the I&A value of $601,834.77 contains all the assets of Decedent's ancillary probate estate. Please amend the account to begin with the I&A value or file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of the account beginning with $1,203,669.54. 5) Please explain and file receipts for all $7,612.38 in alleged owed expenses to Executor. Please address Probate Code 9252. 6) Local Rule 14.57 B says mileage and parking are absorbed as part of fees. It appears the "travel fees" on disbursements totaling $1,747.25 must be paid back to the estate and a receipt filed. 7) Please explain and file a receipt for 01/18/2018 Tenant Search Fees Adam Savick for $2,300.00. 8) The petition alleges the account period ends 08/28/2018. The 11/04/2021 Declaration has a bank statement from April 22, 2016 to May 20, 2016. Please verify the balance at the end of the account period. See LRSF 14.34 D. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/31/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MATTHEW SHUI SANG MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/14/2022. Continued again same issue. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please prove the estate is entirely community property of Decedent. 2) Decedent died 08/29/2016. Unclear when account begins as page 1 line 21 alleges the account begins 08/28/2016, page 4 line 2 alleges the account begins 08/28/2018, the summary of account alleges the beginning assets on hand on 08/28/2016 and Schedule A alleges beginning assets on hand 08/29/2016. A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. (See LRSF 14.34). 3) Petition must be signed and verified by Petitioner. All pleadings filed in proceedings under the Probate Code must be verified. If two or more persons join in a pleading, it may be verified by any of them. (See CRC 7.103). 4) The petition alleges the I&A value of $601,834.77 contains all the assets of Decedent's ancillary probate estate. Please amend the account to begin with the I&A value or file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of the account beginning with $1,203,669.54. 5) Please explain and file receipts for all $8,477.38 in alleged owed expenses to Executor. Please address Probate Code 9252. 6) Please explain on schedule D - Disbursements Appraisal of 300 Berry St., Unit 1108 for $350.00 and file a receipt. Please address Probate Code 8900 et seq and why this appraisal was necessary when the court appointed a probate referee. 7) Local Rule 14.57 B says mileage and parking are absorbed as part of fees. It appears the "travel fees" on disbursements totaling $1,747.25 must be paid back to the estate and a receipt filed. 8) Please explain and file a receipt for 01/18/2018 Tenant Search Fees Adam Savick for $2,300.00. 9) The petition alleges the account period ends 08/28/2018. The 11/04/2021 Declaration has a bank statement from April 22, 2016 to May 20, 2016. Please verify the balance at the end of the account period. See LRSF 14.34 D. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/31/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT E. NETTLETON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Preliminary Distribution [Probate Code 11600 Et Seq.]; And For Of Compensation Of Fifty Percent (50%) Of Estimated Statutory Fees To Executor And Her Attorneys [Probate Code 10800; 10810; 10803]
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/01/2022 by judicial officer. 1. No proposed order has been received or lodged. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2. Please provide the legal basis for how the court can grant the distribution to Kevin M. Legnon of $1,016.12 or $873.26 (amount unclear as Schedule 1 and petition have different amounts) when it appears he has assigned over $89,391.12. Please explain why Kevin should receive any distribution. 3. Explain why the petition has not been amended to address new assignments by Cathy and David. 4. Please address if the assignments are effective under Probate Code 11604.5. Court may take this petition off calendar unless above issues are fully addressed before next calling An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/16/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GLENCHER FAMILY LIVING TRUST DATED JULY 30, 1996
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instruction And Order To Establish Ownership Of Real Property; Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The 1999 grant deed for the Fresno property identified the subject trust as grantee without specifying any person as trustee. The petition alleges that the 1999 deed is null and void for lack of trustees, "causing the ownership of the property to revert back to the settlors as individuals". The petition requests an order transferring record title to the Fresno property from the settlors as individuals to the currently acting co-trustees of their trust. According to Chicago Title Company's title report on file, title to the Fresno property is vested in "Glenchur Family Living Trust dated July 30, 1996", not the settlors as individuals. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 11th Amended General Order which took effect on 04/25/2022 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUBY MAE HOWARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the decedent's son, Norman Howard, should be appointed as the administrator of her estate with limited IAEA authority and $20,000 bond. The Waiver of Bond filed on 3/23/22 waives bond for Debra Lannette McKee, not for the petitioner. (Counsel is encouraged to read item 4 of the Waiver of Bond carefully.) Both documents filed on 3/23/22 were signed electronically by Debra McKee. Does the Court wish to accept the electronically signed nomination? No petition has been filed yet by the (presumed) proponents of the lost will. The lost will lodged with the Court and attached to a "Declaration" filed by DeCoty Phillips and Vivian Gray on 3/1/22 is, therefore, NOT before the Court. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the courtâs website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: 006Deeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.
